task_completion.description: Open the Graphite Dashboard web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web interface is visible in a web browser, showing the dashboard layout.

task_completion.description: Add the CPU utilization metric graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph panel is present in the dashboard with the legend, title, or query explicitly showing 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'.

task_completion.description: Add the disk write bytes metric graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second graph panel is present in the dashboard with the legend, title, or query explicitly showing 'servers.ec2_instance_1.disk.write_bytes'.
The text 'Server Monitoring' is visible in the dashboard title area at the top of the screen or inside the save dialog input field.
A visual confirmation that the dashboard is saved, such as a 'Dashboard Saved' notification, the save dialog disappearing after clicking save, or the URL updating to reflect the saved dashboard name.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Graphite GUI in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser with the Graphite UI, not terminal commands (like curl) or API scripts used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual time-series data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panels should show plotted lines or data points, indicating that the metrics were successfully queried from the backend, rather than being empty or mocked images.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write a JSON dashboard configuration file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard creation must happen within the Graphite web interface, not by editing text files in an IDE or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CPU utilization metric ('servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization') and disk write metric ('servers.ec2_instance_1.disk.write_bytes') are populated with real-world time-series data from the NAB dataset. The VLM should expect to see actual data lines plotted on the graphs, confirming the metrics are correctly querying the underlying real data.
